Details:
Removal of acetochlor by adsorptive media can be effective and is dependent on the contaminant properties (chemical structure, hydrophobicity) and the surface charge of the media. One study showed a removal range of 52 to 93 percent, based on varying concentrations of herbicide, clay, and organic cation surfactant. The same study reported acetochlor removal of greater than 99 percent in a column filter comprised of layers of organoclay complex and quartz sand. Another bench-scale study found acetochlor removal in the range of 11 to 79 percent, depending on the type of organic cation and the level of saturation of surfactant on the clay surface.
March 2009
